response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically strong individual contributor with over 15 years of software development experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and developer tooling domains. His work on CI/CD infrastructure, open-source test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and MCP developer tooling at re:cinq aligns well with the technical focus areas of the role. However, this is explicitly a full-time people-leadership position requiring 2+ years of people management experience, and there is no evidence in the resume of managing direct reports, conducting performance reviews, hiring, or any other people-management responsibilities. His entire career trajectory is that of a senior/staff individual contributor, making him a poor fit for a Level II Engineering Manager role regardless of his technical depth.
strengths: Strong developer tooling background: CI/CD infrastructure, test automation frameworks, MCP code generators, and open-source test runners directly relevant to engineering productivity.
Broad technical depth across full-stack, mobile (iOS/Apple TV), embedded, and systems work, supporting the hands-on coding and system design expectations of the role.
Demonstrated technical leadership through community workshops, conference talks, and staff-level roles, indicating ability to influence and guide engineers.
concerns: Zero evidence of people management: no mention of direct reports, 1:1s, performance management, hiring, or team-building across 15+ years of career history.
No indication of organizational change leadership or roadmap ownership at a team or org level — all experience is individual contributor scope.
No mobile platform ecosystem depth at scale (e.g., Android/iOS build systems, large mobile release pipelines), which is a preferred qualifier for this specific role.
